classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.

The Macedonians hold a Synod at Lampsacus, during a Period of Both Secular and Ecclesiastical Agitation; and after confirming the Antiochian Creed, and anathematizing that promulgated at Ariminum, they again ratify the Deposition of Acacius and Eudoxius.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: The Macedonians hold a Synod at Lampsacus, during a Period of Both Secular and Ecclesiastical Agitation; and after confirming the Antiochian Creed, and anathematizing that promulgated at Ariminum, they again ratify the Deposition of Acacius and Eudoxius.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: While these events were taking place there could be no peace either in the church or in the state.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Now those who had been empowered by the emperor to hold a council assembled at Lampsacus in the consulate just mentioned: this was seven years after the council of Seleucia.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: There, after confirming the Antiochian Creed, to which they had subscribed at Seleucia, they anathematized that which had been set forth at Ariminum by their former associates in opinion.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: They moreover again condemned the party of Acacius and Eudoxius, and declared their deposition to have been just.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The civil war which was then impending prevented Eudoxius bishop of Constantinople from either gainsaying or revenging these determinations.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Wherefore Eleusius bishop of Cyzicus and his adherents became for a little while the stronger party; inasmuch as they supported the views of Macedonius, which although before but obscurely known, acquired great publicity through the Synod at Lampsacus.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: This Synod, I think, was the cause of the increase of the Macedonians in the Hellespont; for Lampsacus is situated in one of the narrow bays of the Hellespont.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Such was the issue of this council.
